title: Measles Virus TaqMan RT-PCR Protocol
author: Mitchell Finger
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.8b9hsr6
materials: SuperScript™ III Platinum™ One-Step qRT-PCR Kit

steps.title: SuperScript™ III Platinum™ One-Step qRT-PCR Kit Life Technologies Catalog #11732088
steps.instruction: SuperScript™ III Platinum™ One-Step qRT-PCR Kit Life Technologies Catalog #11732088

steps.title: The assay has been used on...
steps.instruction: The assay has been used on both a Rotor-Gene 6000 and a Rotor-Gene Q real-time thermocycler Prepare sufficient mix for the number of reactions. Include a suitable 'dead volume' as necessary if using a robotic dispenser. %s %s MIX PREPARATION Reagent Volume (µl) x1 Final reaction concentration Nuclease-free water 4.45 N/A Measles MGB FP 150pmol/µl 0.04 300nM Measles MGB RP 150pmol/µl 0.04 300nM Measles MGB Probe 100pmol/µl 0.03 155nM 2X Reaction Mix1 10 1X SuperScript® III/Platinum® Taq Mix1 0.4 1X ROX Reference Dye (25µM) 0.04 0.05µM Template 5 N/A TOTAL 20 Dispense 15µL to each reaction well. Add 5µL of template (extracted RNA, controls or NTC [nuclease-free water] ). Total reaction volume is 20µL

substeps.instruction: The assay has been used on both a Rotor-Gene 6000 and a Rotor-Gene Q real-time thermocycler Prepare sufficient mix for the number of reactions. Include a suitable 'dead volume' as necessary if using a robotic dispenser. %s %s MIX PREPARATION Reagent Volume (µl) x1 Final reaction concentration Nuclease-free water
155nM 2X Reaction Mix1 10 1X SuperScript® III/Platinum® Taq Mix1
0.05µM Template 5 N/A TOTAL 20 Dispense 15µL to each reaction well. Add 5µL of template (extracted RNA, controls or NTC [nuclease-free water] ). Total reaction volume is 20µL

steps.title: The definition used for a satisfactory...
steps.instruction: The definition used for a satisfactory positive result from a real-time fluorogenic PCR should include each of the following: A sigmoidal curve – the trace travels horizontally, curves upward, continues in an exponential rise and followed by a curve towards a horizontal plateau phase A suitable level of fluorescence intensity as measured in comparison to a positive control (y-axis) A defined threshold (C T ) value which the fluorescent curve has clearly exceeded (Fig.1 arrow), which sits early in the log-linear phase and is <40 cycles A flat or non-sigmoidal curve or a curve that crosses the threshold with a C T >40 cycles is considered a negative result. NTCs should not produce a curve a Figure 1 . Examples of satisfactory sigmoidal amplification curve shape when considering an assay’s fluorescent signal output. The crossing point or threshold cycle (C T ) is indicated (yellow arrow); it is the value at which fluorescence levels surpass a predefined (usually set during validation, or arbitrary) threshold level as shown in this normalized linear scale depiction. LP-log-linear phase of signal generated during the exponential part of the PCR amplification; TP-a slowing of the amplification and accompanying fluorescence signal marks the transition phase; PP-the plateau phase is reached when there is little or no increase in fluorescent signal despite continued cycling.
